page: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is a virtual server belonging to the High Performance family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome architecture. The processor operates at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation model. Memory capacity is 16.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2869.78. Multi-core performance is average, with stress-ng multi-core scoring 22445.91 and PassMark CPU scoring 16668.2. Memory bandwidth is consistent across workloads, registering 923.27 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 84.3 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads. Cryptographic and compression tasks perform in the middle tier, as shown by a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 25.37 GB/sec and a PassMark encryption score of 9428.32.

The shared CPU allocation model introduces potential resource contention, which represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained maximum compute capacity. However, the inclusion of 350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage alongside 16.0 GB of RAM offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose operations. This configuration delivers average performance across database operations, static web serving, and small-scale language model inference, making it a cost-efficient option for web serving, Redis caching, and lightweight database workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
description: The Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server from the High Performance family, featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 350 GB NVMe SSD. While the shared CPU allocation presents potential resource contention tradeoffs, the server demonstrates strong single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks, ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core compute, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. With a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core and no GPU acceleration, this instance is configured for workloads such as static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose database operations.
og_description: Vultr vhp-8c-16gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 350 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core benchmark performance.

tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC virtual server with local NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
350 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and database operations
